reasoning: The FRED statistical tool gives a combined estimate of P(exceed threshold) = 0.30 for the short term, based on a linear trend (-0.002 per day slope) and seasonal analysis. I am adopting this as my primary anchor for the nearer date. However, financial metrics like the 30-year Treasury yield exhibit behavior approximating a random walk over time, meaning that predictive power and the influence of a short-term linear trend deteriorate over longer horizons. Therefore, in accordance with standard forecasting principles, uncertainty must increase as the forecast horizon extends, with probabilities reverting toward the uninformative prior of 0.50. I have scaled the probability of an increase from 0.33 for the 1-week horizon (November 16), up to 0.40 for the 1-month horizon (December 9), and 0.46 for the 3-month horizon (February 7).
